title: `h5i.get_name` has unchecked malloc
author: devdanzin
body:
`malloc(sizeof(char)*(namelen+1))` passed to `H5Iget_name` without NULL check. Segfault when getting an HDF5 object name under OOM.
- `h5i.pyx:101`

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body: just to make sure I understand, you mean "unchecked" as is "not checked for NULL pointers" here, right ?

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body:
(and if so, would this be the appropriate fix ?)
```patch
diff --git a/h5py/h5i.templ.pyx b/h5py/h5i.templ.pyx
index b4f08e51..b6136ee8 100644
--- a/h5py/h5i.templ.pyx
+++ b/h5py/h5i.templ.pyx
@@ -101,6 +101,8 @@ def get_name(ObjectID obj not None):

     assert namelen > 0
     name = <char*>malloc(sizeof(char)*(namelen+1))
+    if name == NULL:
+        return None
     try:
         H5Iget_name(obj.id, name, namelen+1)
         pystring = name
```

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
> just to make sure I understand, you mean "unchecked" as is "not checked for NULL pointers" here, right ?

Correct.

> (and if so, would this be the appropriate fix ?)

I think that, since it's specifically a `malloc` failure, raising a `MemoryError` would be better. Even more so if the caller doesn't expect `None`, which would move the error location far from its origin.
